what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG line icon showing a notification bell with a diagonal slash, using currentColor strokes and no fill.
observations: Root svg uses xmlns, viewBox="0 0 24 24", fill="none", and stroke="currentColor".
Stroke styling is set to width 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
The icon is built from three path elements: partial bell outline, lower bell/right side, and a diagonal slash from top-left to bottom-right.
No title, desc, role, aria attributes, classes, ids, or script content are present.
standouts: Diagonal slash communicates the off or muted state.
conventions: Uses common SVG icon attributes for scalable line icons.
Keeps geometry inline in path d attributes.
Uses rounded stroke caps and joins for softened line endpoints.
concerns: No embedded accessibility label or decorative-hiding attribute is present if used standalone.
notes: A small currentColor SVG line icon for a bell-off notification state.
questions: Should this SVG be treated as decorative or exposed with an accessible label by its consumer?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename implies a bell-off icon, and the SVG content depicts a bell with a diagonal slash.
intention_signals: It appears intended as a reusable interface icon that can inherit surrounding text color while representing disabled or muted notifications.
contribution: Contributes a compact muted-notification bell glyph with simple stroke-based geometry and inherited-color behavior for an icon set.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 currentColor SVG line icon of a notification bell crossed by a diagonal slash.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a bell-off, muted, or disabled notification state in an interface.
